title: Evangelion Anatomy Rebuild Edition Updated 4 24 16
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/24/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Mar 21, 2016 9:12 pm Continuing a tangent that started in another thread. I wanted to take a look at the Evangelion creature under the armor and see how they compare and contrast to each other and what we know about the original Evas. I'll go in order starting with Unit 00: The only glimpse we get of the real Unit 00 is at the end of 1.0, when part of its armor has been burned off by Ramiel's positron beam. We can see the ends of its hands and some swollen, bubbling flesh in the cracks of the knee joints: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 22, 2016 12:53 pm Hah, never expected to see anyone else give this level of attention to Evangelion anatomy. Love it! Having been a studious student of the original Evangelions and every unofficial variation I could find, it was hard to avoid the conclusion that anatomical consistency was very low on the list of priorities. This doesn't seem to have changed with the shift to higher production values, sadly. Ultimately, the fan has little recourse but to imagine something that makes sense to them, based on the bits and pieces glimpsed. In that sense, detailed breakdowns like yours are definitely useful, since they attempt to collect all of the scattered data in one place. Some notes: Eva-00 - We see 00 blocking the Angel's beam with the backs of her hands, so in all likelihood the knuckle dusters were vaporized. - Interesting bit of trivia: Eva-00's core is what Eva-01 retrieves from the 10th Angel. Eva-01 - Teeth of bandaged head are visible in the genga. We can also see, for once, a distinct tear duct. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Mar 22, 2016 6:57 pm Aww, thanks! The Evas and the mysteries surrounded them are my favorite parts of Evangelion. They deserve the attention! That's the impression that I got too, that the animators had a general idea what the Eva would look like and just winged it from there. I think it reaches the peak in End of Evangelion. Poor Unit 02 has three different head designs in the span of ten minutes! Holy crap! Unit 01 looks even creepier with that gap-toothed derp grin. What an excellent shot. You can't even see the contours of the nose/lip in the on-screen version. The eyes are a little farther apart and the teeth are more recessed, but it really does resemble those Adam-things from Until you come to me . Thanks for that! It looks like the forehead is more rounded off than the NGE version. No pseudo-Angel mask? I debated whether or not to include this page from the CRC: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Apr 03, 2016 1:39 pm Updated the main post with new information on Units 01, 02, 06, 09, 13 and completely new entries for Mark.04A, 04B, Unit Null and the Eva Simulator!

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 03, 2016 4:18 pm Quick observation: Eva-02's chest plate is still there where she goes into deep freeze. (You can see it in the right-most CG model on that CRC page.) Though here, again, we see that "color everything we don't feel like detailing black" trope that I mentioned earlier -- perhaps the most egregious example.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Apr 03, 2016 4:38 pm Isn't that the red tip of the shoulder pylon sticking out?

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 03, 2016 4:54 pm Yes. Yes, it is.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Apr 04, 2016 11:37 am Great thread. A suggestion to add: when Shinji is placed in charge of firing at Ramiel and Unit 01 is being outfitted with the sniping gear, the mask it's being fitted with looked very suspicious to me. It had several eyes(11? 12?). Might be worth examining the anatomy.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Chroma » Mon Apr 04, 2016 1:58 pm Great posts, man. The information is laid out really nice. I do have a question about Null though - when did it get 'revealed'? I hadn't heard about it til now. Do we have any other info on it aside from that sketch?

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Mon Apr 04, 2016 2:01 pm In the short film Evangelion: Another Impact directed by Shinji Aramaki and shown at the Japan animator expo

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 04, 2016 2:38 pm unitM wrote: Great thread. A suggestion to add: when Shinji is placed in charge of firing at Ramiel and Unit 01 is being outfitted with the sniping gear, the mask it's being fitted with looked very suspicious to me. It had several eyes(11? 12?). Might be worth examining the anatomy.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Apr 04, 2016 11:35 pm Reichu wrote: The sniping gear was basically just a pylon attachment, if memory serves. Eva-01's mask remains the same throughout the film, even after it is destroyed and replaced. I'm not sure what you're referring to here.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 05, 2016 12:58 am What's dangling there in the screenshot you posted is one of at least two similar-looking components in the body of the positron cannon (evidenced by looking at the concept art in 1.0 CRC). There's some chickenscratch that seems to identify it as a "receiver"; the lens-looking things are probably antennae. Has nothing to do with any of the equipment worn by Eva-01, anyway.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Apr 06, 2016 6:05 pm Thanks everyone! Chroma wrote: Great posts, man. The information is laid out really nice. I do have a question about Null though - when did it get 'revealed'? I hadn't heard about it til now. Do we have any other info on it aside from that sketch?

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/3/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Apr 24, 2016 7:30 pm Since this thread is already delving into the nitty gritty of the Eva Units, I'll go ahead and bump this instead of making a new topic. I've been wanting to take a look at the distinction between the Mark Evas and the Unit Evas to see if we can't determine what separates the two. The movie offers no commentary on the matter, but I've been able to pick out a few traits that are common to the Mark sub-series. 1. Internal Power Source- Mark.06, Mark.09, and the three variants of Mark.04 all operate with no umbilical cables or any other connections to external power sources. I assume Marks.06 and 09 posses S2 Engines/Fruits of Life being Adam-derived life forms. Mark.04 might also have them, but since Wille is using them as N2 reactors for the Wunder, it's possible they have working versions of the internal engine that was being tested on the lost Unit 04. 2. Flight- All five Evas demonstrate the ability to fly without the aid of equipment. Mark.06 manifests a halo and flies like some of the Angels do. Mark.09 uses that huge booster rocket in its introduction, but once it sheds the Mark.09 guise and transforms into the Vessel of the Adams, it's able to fly in the same way as Mark.06. 4A pushes itself with some kind of AT Field, while B and C just kind of hover. 3. Autonomous Operation- The Eva is able to operate without a pilot. The Vessel of the Adams switches over to some kind of Seele OS, breaks synchronization, overrides Rei's controls and begins acting apparently of its own accord. It continues this way after Rei ejects, so it's not using a Dummy Plug. The Mark.04 Units are unmanned drones and have no visible ports or access points for an Entry Plug. We never see Mark.06 acting without a pilot, but Kaworu says it was modified to have that ability before its death. There aren't any further details, but it's possible that Mark.06 was the first Eva to use whatever is running Marks.09 and 04. Unit 01 meets conditions 1 and 2 after it awakens, but not 3. It is completely dormant and refuses to move without Shinji on board. It rejects the Dummy Plug outright when Gendo tries to give it independence. The Unit 01 from NGE does meet all three criteria after End of Evangelion. It really puzzled me why Evangelion 13 wasn't a Mark. It has unlimited internal power, and it unlocks flight mode after seizing the Spears of Longinus. I don't think it meets condition 3. Shinji's controls are overridden by something and the Eva at first appears to be acting on its own, but once Kaworu dies and Shinji is out, it completely powers down and crashes to the Earth. The Spears might have had something to do with that, but it's weird that it wasn't forced into dormancy before Shinji ejected. The Mark units also have unusual methods of construction and deviate from the standard Production Model design (fleet of flying saucers, elongated torsos), but with Units 01, 05, 13 and the other weirdness in the Eva series, I don't think that should be counted as a defining characteristic.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/24/16)             Quote Post by Joseki » Wed Mar 22, 2017 3:05 pm Just saw this topic. Fantastic work BlueBasilisk, the pictures are amazing and there's some stuff I didn't noticed until now. Regarding the Mark or Eva nomenclature I thought I could variate based on the manufacturer: with the exception of Unit-05 that is manufactured by IPEA I think that all the "Eva" units are made by NERV (or IPEA and NERV actually workedtogether on the manufacturing as groups of the UN, I don't know), "Mark" units are instead all manufactured and controlled directly by SEELE. Unit 00 and 01 we know were devoleped by NERV JP, Unit 02 by NERV GER, Unit 03 and 04 by NERV US. Unit 05 was in a NERV base too but as I mentioned it was shown in a NERV but it was manufactured by IPEA. Unit 13 is again under Gendo's control. Unit 8 is "just there" by I suppose is not something created by SEELE. Mark 6 is manufactured on the Moon and Gendo doesn't get permission to land from SEELE, Mark 9 has a SEELE OS onboard. Mark 4A/B/C are something totally different from the other Eva/Mark units but I tend to think them as a creation of the SEELE. I also have a theory about Eva-01 and Eva-00 being originated from the same Adam: At the end of 2.0 Unit-01 reach phase 2 of its pseudo evolved form after having absorbed the core of Unit-00 from the Angel. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/24/16)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 22, 2017 4:29 pm Out of curiosity, why were Units 05 and 08 excluded?

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/24/16)             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Mar 22, 2017 5:05 pm I had a reason at the time, but it's been so long that I can't remember what it was. In Unit 08's case, I think I excluded it because it's always fully armored and we never get a peek at what's underneath.

[]: Re: Evangelion Anatomy - Rebuild Edition (Updated 4/24/16)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 23, 2017 9:00 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: I had a reason at the time, but it's been so long that I can't remember what it was. In Unit 08's case, I think I excluded it because it's always fully armored and we never get a peek at what's underneath.
